task_completion.description: Partition walls and at least 4 doors are added to divide the floor plan into distinct zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for newly drawn interior walls on the 2D floor plan and at least 4 door icons/models placed within or intersecting these walls.

task_completion.description: At least 3 treatment operatories are created, each containing a dental chair, a cabinet or shelf unit, and a lamp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 3 distinct rooms or partitioned areas on the plan, each containing a dental chair (or similar reclining chair model), a storage unit (cabinet/shelf), and a lighting fixture (lamp).

task_completion.description: The reception area has a desk, at least 6 waiting chairs, and 2 side tables. The sterilization zone has at least 3 shelf units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a waiting area containing a desk, 6 or more chairs, and 2 or more small tables. Look for another distinct area (sterilization) containing 3 or more shelving units.

task_completion.description: The staff break room contains a table, chairs, and at least 1 appliance. The facility includes at least 2 toilets and 2 sinks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a break room area with a table, seating, and an appliance (e.g., microwave, refrigerator, coffee maker). Look for restroom areas containing a total of at least 2 toilets and 2 sinks across the floor plan.

task_completion.description: At least 5 distinct zones are identified with text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text elements placed on the 2D floor plan labeling at least 5 different rooms or areas (e.g., 'Operatory 1', 'Reception', 'Sterilization', 'Staff Break Room', 'Restroom').

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window open with the floor plan being actively edited, rather than a terminal or text editor modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: Furniture and structural elements are placed logically within the boundaries of the building shell.
integrity.visual_evidence: Items should be placed inside the floor plan walls, not scattered randomly outside the building or overlapping in impossible ways.

integrity.description: The agent saved the changes to the project file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the save icon being clicked, the 'File -> Save' menu being accessed, or the absence of an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename 'dental_office_starter.sh3d' in the title bar) in the final screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying 'dental_office_starter.sh3d' in Sweet Home 3D. The VLM should verify the presence of: 1) New partition walls and >=4 doors. 2) 3 operatories, each with a dental chair, cabinet/shelf, and lamp. 3) Reception area with a desk, >=6 chairs, and >=2 side tables. 4) Sterilization zone with >=3 shelf units. 5) Break room with a table, chairs, and >=1 appliance. 6) Restrooms with >=2 toilets and >=2 sinks. 7) >=5 text labels identifying zones on the floor plan. The total score is 100, with a passing threshold of 70.
